title: RRR
cast: N. T. Rama Rao Jr., Ram Charan, Alia Bhatt, Ajay Devgn
overview: Set during British colonial rule in India, the story follows two revolutionaries whose lives become unexpectedly intertwined before they unite against oppression. One fights to rescue a young tribal girl taken away by the British while the other secretly works within the colonial system for a larger mission. Their friendship, emotional conflicts, sacrifices, and explosive rebellion create a grand narrative about freedom, loyalty, courage, and resistance. The film blends intense action, patriotic emotion, large-scale spectacle, and deeply personal struggles while exploring brotherhood and the price of revolution.

title: Pushpa: The Rise
cast: Allu Arjun, Rashmika Mandanna, Fahadh Faasil
overview: Pushpa Raj, a daily wage laborer from a poor background, enters the dangerous world of red sandalwood smuggling in the forests of South India. Through intelligence, aggression, and fearless ambition, he rises through the ranks of a brutal criminal network controlled by powerful smugglers and corrupt officials. As Pushpa gains influence, he faces betrayal, rivalry, class discrimination, and violent confrontations that challenge his survival and identity. The film explores ambition, masculinity, social hierarchy, greed, and power while presenting a gritty underworld filled with tension and raw emotion.

title: Kantara
cast: Rishab Shetty, Sapthami Gowda, Kishore
overview: In a coastal village deeply connected to divine folklore and ancestral traditions, tensions rise between local communities, forest officers, and influential landowners. Shiva, a rebellious young villager, becomes entangled in a conflict involving sacred customs, spiritual rituals, and the struggle to protect the land tied to generations of belief. As mysterious events unfold, ancient traditions collide with modern authority, forcing Shiva to confront his identity and destiny. The film combines mythology, spirituality, environmental conflict, action, and emotional drama while exploring faith, fear, pride, and the relationship between humans and nature.

title: KGF: Chapter 2
cast: Yash, Sanjay Dutt, Raveena Tandon, Srinidhi Shetty
overview: After taking control of the Kolar Gold Fields, Rocky becomes one of the most feared men in the criminal world. His growing influence attracts dangerous enemies including political leaders, rival gangs, and the ruthless Adheera, who seeks revenge and domination. While the people of KGF view Rocky as a savior, his hunger for power pushes him deeper into violence and conflict. The film explores ambition, dominance, loyalty, revenge, sacrifice, and the burden of leadership through large-scale action sequences and emotionally charged confrontations.

title: Jawan
cast: Shah Rukh Khan, Nayanthara, Vijay Sethupathi, Deepika Padukone
overview: A mysterious vigilante hijacks public systems and challenges corruption across the nation while carrying deep emotional scars from his past. As he targets powerful businessmen and political figures responsible for injustice, hidden truths emerge about sacrifice, family, and betrayal. The story unfolds through high-energy action, emotional drama, social commentary, and psychological conflict. The film examines themes of corruption, responsibility, patriotism, revenge, and redemption while balancing mass entertainment with emotional storytelling.

title: Pathaan
cast: Shah Rukh Khan, Deepika Padukone, John Abraham
overview: A skilled Indian spy returns from exile to stop a deadly terrorist attack planned by a former agent driven by revenge against his own country. As global conspiracies, advanced weapons, and dangerous missions unfold, Pathaan teams up with allies to prevent catastrophic destruction. The film delivers fast-paced action, espionage, betrayal, emotional conflict, and international adventure while exploring themes of patriotism, sacrifice, trust, and resilience.

title: Drishyam 2
cast: Mohanlal, Meena, Ansiba Hassan
overview: Years after successfully protecting his family from a murder investigation, Georgekutty lives under constant fear and suspicion as old secrets begin resurfacing. When the police reopen the case with renewed determination, Georgekutty must once again rely on intelligence, preparation, and psychological strategy to safeguard his loved ones. The film builds tension through layered storytelling, emotional conflict, and carefully constructed suspense while exploring morality, guilt, family protection, and deception.

title: Minnal Murali
cast: Tovino Thomas, Guru Somasundaram
overview: After being struck by lightning during a strange natural event, a small-town tailor discovers he has developed extraordinary powers. While struggling to understand his new abilities and responsibilities, he faces another man whose tragic life transforms him into a dangerous antagonist with similar powers. Set in a grounded rural environment, the film combines superhero action with emotional storytelling, humor, human vulnerability, and social realism while exploring loneliness, morality, and heroism.

title: Sita Ramam
cast: Dulquer Salmaan, Mrunal Thakur, Rashmika Mandanna
overview: An orphaned army officer serving near the Kashmir border receives heartfelt letters from a mysterious woman named Sita, leading to a deep emotional connection that changes his life forever. Years later, another young woman becomes involved in uncovering the hidden truth behind their relationship and a forgotten letter that carries emotional significance. The film blends romance, war, sacrifice, longing, and emotional destiny while exploring love across time, trust, separation, and personal redemption.

title: Hi Nanna
cast: Nani, Mrunal Thakur, Kiara Khanna
overview: A caring single father shares a beautiful bond with his young daughter while hiding painful truths from his past. When a woman unexpectedly enters their lives, emotional wounds, forgotten memories, and unresolved relationships slowly begin resurfacing. The story unfolds through emotional conversations, family warmth, romantic conflict, and personal healing. The film explores love, parenting, forgiveness, emotional trauma, and second chances through gentle storytelling and heartfelt performances.

title: Master
cast: Vijay, Vijay Sethupathi, Malavika Mohanan
overview: An alcoholic college professor with a rebellious personality is sent to a juvenile correction facility where he uncovers a violent system manipulated by a ruthless gangster who exploits children for criminal activities. As he forms connections with the students, he becomes determined to fight against corruption and abuse despite personal weaknesses and emotional struggles. The film combines action, social commentary, redemption, and psychological conflict while examining power, manipulation, and responsibility.

title: Kaithi
cast: Karthi, Narain, Arjun Das
overview: A recently released prisoner desperately trying to meet his daughter becomes trapped in a deadly night-long mission involving poisoned police officers, hidden drug cartels, and violent criminals. Forced to assist the police despite his troubled past, he must survive relentless attacks while protecting innocent lives. The film unfolds almost entirely over one tense night and blends emotional drama, survival, suspense, and high-intensity action while exploring fatherhood, redemption, and sacrifice.

title: Vikram
cast: Kamal Haasan, Vijay Sethupathi, Fahadh Faasil
overview: A series of brutal murders involving masked vigilantes leads a black-ops investigator into a dangerous criminal network tied to drugs, corruption, and hidden identities. As the investigation deepens, long-buried secrets about a supposedly dead former agent begin surfacing. The film blends espionage, emotional revenge, undercover operations, and intense action while exploring justice, sacrifice, fatherhood, and the consequences of violence in a dark criminal world.

title: Leo
cast: Vijay, Trisha, Sanjay Dutt
overview: A quiet café owner living peacefully with his family in Himachal Pradesh becomes the target of dangerous gangsters who believe he is connected to a violent criminal from their past. As his hidden history slowly unfolds, brutal confrontations and emotional conflicts threaten to destroy his carefully built life. The film combines family drama, psychological tension, action, and crime while exploring identity, trauma, violence, and survival.

title: Jersey
cast: Nani, Shraddha Srinath, Sathyaraj
overview: A talented but failed cricketer struggling with unemployment and personal disappointment decides to return to professional cricket in his thirties to fulfill his young son’s wish. Battling age, criticism, financial pressure, and emotional insecurity, he begins a difficult journey toward redemption and self-respect. The film emotionally explores fatherhood, dreams, sacrifice, perseverance, and the pain of unrealized potential through grounded storytelling and inspiring sports drama.

title: Maharaja
cast: Vijay Sethupathi, Mamta Mohandas, Anurag Kashyap
overview: A quiet barber walks into a police station demanding help in finding a stolen object he calls 'Lakshmi,' but the strange complaint slowly reveals a deeply emotional and violent mystery connected to his past. As hidden truths emerge through nonlinear storytelling, the investigation exposes trauma, revenge, grief, and shocking human cruelty. The film combines psychological tension, emotional drama, crime investigation, and moral complexity while keeping the audience uncertain about the real story until the final moments.

title: Aavesham
cast: Fahadh Faasil, Hipzster, Mithun Jai Shankar
overview: A group of college students seeking revenge after being humiliated by local seniors unexpectedly become associated with a flamboyant gangster known for his unpredictable behavior and explosive personality. As friendship, violence, comedy, and chaos spiral together, the students are drawn deeper into the criminal world. The film balances dark humor, emotional unpredictability, energetic action, and eccentric character moments while exploring loneliness, loyalty, masculinity, and influence.

title: Manjummel Boys
cast: Soubin Shahir, Sreenath Bhasi, Balu Varghese
overview: A group of close friends from Kerala travel to Kodaikanal for a vacation that turns into a terrifying survival mission when one of them falls into a dangerous hidden cave with almost no chance of rescue. As fear, guilt, friendship, and desperation intensify, the group risks everything to save their companion. Inspired by real events, the film explores brotherhood, courage, trauma, and hope while building tension through emotional realism and survival drama.

title: 777 Charlie
cast: Rakshit Shetty, Sangeetha Sringeri
overview: A lonely and emotionally disconnected factory worker living a repetitive life unexpectedly forms a deep bond with a stray dog named Charlie. As Charlie transforms his routine and emotional world, the pair embark on a heartfelt journey across the country filled with friendship, healing, humor, and painful truths. The film explores loneliness, companionship, emotional recovery, and unconditional love while presenting an emotional road-trip narrative centered around human-animal connection.

title: Kumbalangi Nights
cast: Soubin Shahir, Shane Nigam, Fahadh Faasil
overview: Four emotionally distant brothers living in a dysfunctional household struggle with poverty, responsibility, toxic masculinity, and personal identity in a small coastal town. As relationships slowly develop and emotional wounds surface, the brothers are forced to confront their insecurities and learn the meaning of family and acceptance. The film combines realism, subtle humor, romance, and psychological depth while exploring masculinity, emotional vulnerability, and human connection.

title: Soorarai Pottru
cast: Suriya, Aparna Balamurali, Paresh Rawal
overview: Inspired by real events, the film follows an ambitious young man from a rural background who dreams of making air travel affordable for ordinary people. Despite financial struggles, social humiliation, and powerful corporate opposition, he relentlessly fights to build a low-cost airline that can change millions of lives. The story explores ambition, innovation, sacrifice, marriage, friendship, and resilience while highlighting the emotional and economic barriers faced by dreamers challenging established systems.

title: Tumbbad
cast: Sohum Shah, Mohammad Samad, Jyoti Malshe
overview: Set across several decades in a rain-soaked village haunted by mythological greed, the story follows a man obsessed with discovering a hidden treasure connected to an ancient cursed deity. As his ambition grows stronger, he repeatedly risks his family, morality, and sanity in pursuit of limitless wealth hidden beneath terrifying supernatural forces. The film combines folklore horror, psychological tension, period storytelling, and dark fantasy while exploring greed, inheritance, fear, and the destructive nature of obsession.

title: Andhadhun
cast: Ayushmann Khurrana, Tabu, Radhika Apte
overview: A talented pianist pretending to be blind unexpectedly becomes trapped inside a dangerous web of murder, deception, blackmail, and manipulation after witnessing a shocking crime. As lies continuously overlap and every character hides secret motives, the situation grows increasingly unpredictable and morally ambiguous. The film combines dark humor, suspense, psychological tension, and crime thriller storytelling while exploring greed, survival, morality, and the uncertainty of truth.

title: 3 Idiots
cast: Aamir Khan, R. Madhavan, Sharman Joshi
overview: Three engineering students struggling under academic pressure, parental expectations, and rigid educational systems develop a life-changing friendship while questioning society’s definition of success. Years later, two of them embark on a journey to rediscover their brilliant and unconventional friend whose philosophy transformed their understanding of learning and happiness. The film combines humor, emotional storytelling, friendship, and social commentary while exploring education, ambition, creativity, mental health, and individuality.

title: ZNMD
cast: Hrithik Roshan, Farhan Akhtar, Abhay Deol
overview: Three childhood friends reunite for a long-delayed road trip across Spain before one of them gets married, but hidden emotional insecurities and unresolved personal conflicts slowly begin surfacing throughout the journey. Through adventure sports, relationships, and honest conversations, each man confronts fear, regret, ambition, and emotional vulnerability. The film combines travel adventure, friendship drama, romance, and self-discovery while exploring adulthood, freedom, emotional healing, and meaningful relationships.

title: Ustad Hotel
cast: Dulquer Salmaan, Thilakan, Nithya Menen
overview: A young man dreaming of becoming a professional chef returns unwillingly to Kerala after conflict with his wealthy father and begins working at his grandfather’s small beachside restaurant. Through simple food, emotional conversations, and interactions with struggling people, he slowly learns compassion, purpose, and the emotional meaning of life beyond money and status. The film combines family drama, culinary storytelling, emotional warmth, and social realism while exploring ambition, humanity, and intergenerational relationships.

title: Pizza
cast: Vijay Sethupathi, Remya Nambeesan
overview: A pizza delivery worker already struggling with financial problems and relationship stress experiences terrifying supernatural events while delivering food to a mysterious house one stormy night. As fear, paranoia, and unexplained incidents intensify, hidden truths surrounding the haunting slowly emerge in unexpected ways. The film combines psychological horror, suspense, emotional tension, and mystery storytelling while exploring fear, survival, deception, and uncertainty.

title: Delhi Belly
cast: Imran Khan, Kunaal Roy Kapur, Vir Das
overview: Three irresponsible roommates living chaotic lives in Delhi accidentally become involved in a dangerous criminal situation involving smuggled diamonds, gangsters, mistaken identities, and constant bad luck. As absurd misunderstandings spiral into violent consequences, the friends struggle desperately to survive while managing their dysfunctional relationships and personal failures. The film combines adult humor, crime thriller elements, urban chaos, and satire while exploring greed, immaturity, friendship, and survival.

title: Ugly
cast: Rahul Bhat, Ronit Roy, Tejaswini Kolhapure
overview: The disappearance of a young girl unexpectedly exposes the selfishness, emotional damage, addiction, and moral corruption hidden within the lives of multiple adults connected to her. As police investigations and personal conflicts intensify, relationships collapse under greed, manipulation, and emotional trauma. The film combines psychological realism, crime investigation, dark emotional drama, and social commentary while exploring human weakness, broken relationships, guilt, and moral decay.

title: October
cast: Varun Dhawan, Banita Sandhu, Gitanjali Rao
overview: An immature hotel management trainee unexpectedly becomes emotionally attached to a quiet coworker after she suffers a devastating accident that leaves her critically injured. As he spends increasing amounts of time caring for her despite lacking any romantic relationship, he slowly begins understanding empathy, emotional responsibility, and silent human connection. The film combines emotional realism, subtle storytelling, and slice-of-life drama while exploring grief, care, loneliness, and unconditional attachment.

title: Kahaani
cast: Vidya Balan, Parambrata Chatterjee, Nawazuddin Siddiqui
overview: A pregnant woman arrives in Kolkata during the busy Durga Puja celebrations searching desperately for her missing husband, but every clue leads deeper into a dangerous conspiracy involving intelligence agencies, terrorism, and hidden identities. As authorities question her story and mysterious deaths begin occurring around the investigation, she continues pursuing the truth with remarkable determination. The film combines suspense, emotional tension, investigative mystery, and psychological manipulation while exploring grief, deception, and revenge.

title: Rock On!!
cast: Farhan Akhtar, Arjun Rampal, Purab Kohli
overview: Four former bandmates who abandoned their musical dreams years earlier reunite after personal tragedy forces them to confront unresolved conflicts, regret, and emotional distance. As old friendships slowly heal through music and emotional honesty, each individual begins reevaluating ambition, relationships, and lost passion. The film combines music, friendship drama, emotional realism, and personal redemption while exploring adulthood, creativity, reconciliation, and second chances.

title: Lucifer
cast: Mohanlal, Vivek Oberoi, Manju Warrier
overview: Following the sudden death of a powerful political leader, hidden power struggles erupt within Kerala’s political and criminal networks as rival groups compete for influence and control. Amid the chaos, a mysterious and highly influential figure operating behind the scenes begins manipulating events in ways nobody fully understands. The film combines political drama, organized crime, emotional family conflict, and action thriller elements while exploring loyalty, power, betrayal, and hidden influence.

title: Bhaag Milkha Bhaag
cast: Farhan Akhtar, Sonam Kapoor, Divya Dutta
overview: Based on the life of legendary athlete Milkha Singh, the film follows a traumatized refugee who survives the horrors of Partition and gradually transforms into one of India’s greatest runners through discipline, pain, and determination. Haunted by childhood memories and personal loss, he channels emotional suffering into relentless athletic ambition. The film combines biography, sports drama, emotional trauma, and historical context while exploring resilience, patriotism, identity, and perseverance.

title: Queen
cast: Kangana Ranaut, Rajkummar Rao, Lisa Haydon
overview: After being abandoned by her fiancé shortly before marriage, a shy young woman decides to continue her planned honeymoon alone across Europe despite fear and emotional heartbreak. Through unexpected friendships, travel experiences, and cultural exposure, she gradually discovers independence, confidence, and personal freedom beyond societal expectations. The film combines humor, emotional healing, travel adventure, and self-discovery while exploring identity, empowerment, loneliness, and personal growth.

cast: Vijay Sethupathi, Trisha Krishnan
overview: A quiet travel photographer revisits his hometown for a school reunion and unexpectedly reconnects with his childhood love after decades of emotional separation. As memories, regrets, and unspoken feelings slowly resurface through intimate conversations over a single night, both individuals reflect on the choices and circumstances that shaped their lives. The film combines nostalgia, emotional realism, subtle romance, and reflective storytelling while exploring memory, longing, timing, and emotional closure.

title: Vikramarkudu
cast: Ravi Teja, Anushka Shetty, Vineet Kumar
overview: A small-time thief living selfishly and carelessly unexpectedly becomes connected to a fearless police officer whose violent mission against a brutal criminal empire threatens powerful enemies. Through mistaken identity and emotional circumstances involving a young child, the thief gradually transforms into someone willing to fight injustice and protect innocent people. The film combines mass action, emotional drama, comedy, and revenge storytelling while exploring courage, redemption, sacrifice, and responsibility.

title: Anbe Sivam
cast: Kamal Haasan, R. Madhavan, Kiran Rathod
overview: Two strangers with completely different personalities and worldviews are forced to travel together across India after an unexpected accident disrupts their plans. During the emotionally transformative journey, stories of love, sacrifice, activism, and personal suffering slowly reveal the compassionate philosophy shaping one man’s life despite immense pain and physical scars. The film combines humor, emotional storytelling, political commentary, and philosophical reflection while exploring humanity, kindness, socialism, and unconditional compassion.

title: Ayan
cast: Suriya, Tamannaah Bhatia, Prabhu
overview: A daring young smuggler working across international borders becomes involved in dangerous conflicts involving illegal trade networks, betrayal, and violent rivalries after entering the world controlled by powerful criminals. As greed, ambition, and emotional loyalty begin colliding, he must navigate life-threatening situations while protecting the people closest to him. The film combines stylish action, international crime, emotional drama, and suspenseful thriller storytelling.

title: Aadukalam
cast: Dhanush, Taapsee Pannu, V. I. S. Jayapalan
overview: A talented rooster-fight trainer living in Madurai shares a complicated relationship with his respected mentor whose pride and insecurity slowly turn admiration into jealousy. As traditional competitions, emotional betrayal, and village politics intensify, the young man becomes trapped in conflict involving loyalty, ambition, and identity. The film combines rural realism, emotional drama, sports culture, and psychological tension while exploring ego, mentorship, masculinity, and betrayal.

title: Soodhu Kavvum
cast: Vijay Sethupathi, Bobby Simha, Ashok Selvan
overview: A small-time criminal with bizarre kidnapping principles teams up with several unemployed young men to execute low-risk abductions for money, but their plans spiral into absurd chaos after political conspiracies and personal greed interfere. As reality and delusion increasingly blur around the eccentric leader, the situation becomes both dangerous and hilarious. The film combines dark comedy, crime satire, suspense, and absurd humor while exploring desperation, corruption, and moral ambiguity.

title: Madras
cast: Karthi, Catherine Tresa, Kalaiyarasan
overview: A politically charged conflict surrounding a symbolic wall in North Chennai deeply affects the lives of local youth trapped between friendship, loyalty, and violent political rivalry. As emotional wounds, revenge, and social frustration intensify, a young man struggles to protect his loved ones while confronting systemic manipulation and power struggles within his community. The film combines urban realism, political drama, emotional friendship, and action while exploring identity, oppression, and social division.

title: Kaaka Muttai
cast: Ramesh, Vignesh, Aishwarya Rajesh
overview: Two young brothers growing up in a Chennai slum become obsessed with tasting pizza after seeing advertisements and wealthy children enjoying it. Their innocent dream slowly exposes the painful economic inequality and social barriers surrounding urban life as they attempt to earn enough money to enter a luxury restaurant. The film combines childhood innocence, humor, emotional realism, and social commentary while exploring poverty, aspiration, dignity, and consumer culture.

title: Maan Karate
cast: Sivakarthikeyan, Hansika Motwani, Vamsi Krishna
overview: A carefree young man unexpectedly receives a mysterious prediction paper revealing future events and learns that he must somehow transform an untrained boxer into a champion fighter. As greed, romance, manipulation, and emotional pressure complicate the journey, he struggles to understand whether destiny can truly be changed. The film combines fantasy, sports entertainment, humor, and emotional drama while exploring ambition, fate, friendship, and self-belief.

title: Kirik Party
cast: Rakshit Shetty, Rashmika Mandanna, Samyuktha Hegde
overview: A shy engineering student enters college life expecting ordinary academic routines but gradually experiences friendship, romance, heartbreak, rebellion, and emotional maturity through unforgettable campus experiences. As relationships evolve and personal tragedies reshape his outlook on life, he slowly transforms from an immature teenager into a more responsible adult. The film combines youthful humor, emotional nostalgia, romance, and coming-of-age storytelling while exploring friendship, identity, and emotional growth.

title: Dia
cast: Kushee Ravi, Dheekshith Shetty, Pruthvi Ambaar
overview: A reserved young woman struggling with emotional trauma and loneliness slowly reconnects with love after painful heartbreak changes the direction of her life. As relationships from the past and present begin overlapping in emotionally devastating ways, she is forced to confront grief, healing, and difficult choices about happiness and sacrifice. The film combines emotional realism, romance, tragedy, and introspective storytelling while exploring memory, loss, and emotional vulnerability.

title: Kavaludaari
cast: Rishi, Anant Nag, Achyuth Kumar
overview: A traffic police officer bored with routine duties becomes obsessed with investigating a decades-old case after human bones are discovered during construction work in Bengaluru. As he uncovers hidden political secrets, forgotten crimes, and emotional truths buried beneath the city’s history, the investigation grows increasingly dangerous. The film combines atmospheric mystery, investigative suspense, emotional storytelling, and noir-style crime drama while exploring obsession, corruption, and memory.

title: Maya
cast: Nayanthara, Aari Arujunan, Lakshmi Priyaa
overview: A struggling single mother accepts an opportunity to act in a horror film but gradually experiences terrifying supernatural events connected to the movie’s disturbing storyline and a tragic past involving death and obsession. As reality and fiction begin merging, she becomes trapped in psychological fear and emotional trauma while trying to protect her child. The film combines atmospheric horror, emotional storytelling, mystery, and psychological suspense while exploring motherhood, fear, and unresolved pain.

title: Kirik Hit
cast: Dhananjay, Samyuktha Hornad
overview: A timid and unlucky salesman unexpectedly becomes trapped inside a dangerous criminal conspiracy after a series of chaotic misunderstandings place him at the center of a murder investigation. Forced to survive increasingly bizarre situations involving gangsters, deception, and hidden agendas, he slowly discovers unexpected courage within himself. The film combines crime comedy, suspense, dark humor, and emotional awkwardness while exploring survival, fear, and accidental heroism.

title: Mahaan
cast: Vikram, Dhruv Vikram, Simran
overview: A middle-aged man who spent decades suppressing his desires under strict family expectations suddenly embraces freedom and rebellion after abandoning his conventional life. His transformation gradually pulls him into the dangerous world of crime, alcohol trade, and power struggles, eventually creating emotional conflict with his idealistic son. The film combines gangster drama, emotional family conflict, political undertones, and psychological transformation while exploring freedom, morality, rebellion, and father-son relationships.

title: Love Mocktail
cast: Darling Krishna, Milana Nagaraj
overview: A heartbroken software engineer reflects on the different stages of love he experienced throughout his life, revisiting relationships that shaped his emotional identity and understanding of happiness. Through memories of friendship, romance, separation, and grief, he gradually confronts loneliness and emotional healing. The film combines emotional realism, romantic nostalgia, and reflective storytelling while exploring heartbreak, companionship, memory, and personal growth.

title: Sarileru Neekevvaru
cast: Mahesh Babu, Rashmika Mandanna, Vijayashanti
overview: An energetic Indian Army major travels to a small town to deliver tragic news to the family of an injured soldier but unexpectedly becomes involved in a dangerous battle against a corrupt politician terrorizing innocent civilians. While balancing emotional responsibility and explosive action, he fights injustice using intelligence, courage, and humor. The film combines military action, family emotion, political conflict, and comedy while exploring patriotism, duty, and social responsibility.

title: Bro Daddy
cast: Mohanlal, Prithviraj Sukumaran, Kalyani Priyadarshan
overview: The peaceful relationship between a father and son becomes hilariously complicated after both families discover unexpected pregnancies that create emotional confusion, generational embarrassment, and hidden secrets. As misunderstandings and social expectations spiral into chaos, family members are forced to confront responsibility, maturity, and communication. The film combines family comedy, emotional warmth, and generational humor while exploring relationships, parenthood, honesty, and acceptance.

title: Leader
cast: Rana Daggubati, Richa Gangopadhyay, Priya Anand
overview: After the sudden death of his powerful politician father, an educated young man unexpectedly becomes Chief Minister and attempts to fight corruption within a deeply compromised political system. As party leaders, businessmen, and criminals resist his reforms, he struggles between idealism and political survival while confronting emotional betrayal and public expectations. The film combines political drama, emotional conflict, and social commentary while exploring governance, ethics, power, and systemic corruption.

title: Athidhi
cast: Mahesh Babu, Amrita Rao, Murali Sharma
overview: A lonely young man haunted by childhood trauma reunites with the girl he once protected after years of separation, but violent enemies connected to his past continue threatening their lives. As hidden conspiracies, emotional pain, and brutal confrontations intensify, he must confront dangerous criminals while protecting the people he loves. The film combines emotional action drama, psychological conflict, suspense, and revenge themes while exploring loss, loyalty, trauma, and redemption.

title: Jalsa
cast: Pawan Kalyan, Ileana D'Cruz, Prakash Raj
overview: A carefree young man with a violent revolutionary past attempts to live peacefully after abandoning extremist ideology, but his history slowly resurfaces through political enemies, emotional relationships, and dangerous conflicts. While balancing humor, romance, and emotional scars, he struggles to reconcile his past actions with his desire for a normal life. The film combines action, political undertones, emotional drama, and comedy while exploring ideology, redemption, and identity.

title: Race Gurram
cast: Allu Arjun, Shruti Haasan, Shaam
overview: A carefree and impulsive young man constantly clashes with his disciplined police officer brother, whose fight against a ruthless politician slowly pulls the entire family into danger. While initially avoiding responsibility, the younger brother gradually transforms into an unexpected ally during the battle against corruption and violence. The film combines energetic comedy, family emotion, mass action, and political conflict while exploring brotherhood, responsibility, and courage.

title: Orange
cast: Ram Charan, Genelia D'Souza, Shazahn Padamsee
overview: A passionate graffiti artist living in Australia deeply believes that lifelong love is unrealistic because human emotions constantly change over time. When he falls in love with an optimistic young woman who dreams of eternal romance, their relationship becomes emotionally complicated through conflicting views about commitment and honesty. The film combines romance, emotional introspection, travel atmosphere, and youthful drama while exploring love, fear, relationships, and emotional insecurity.

title: Vedha
cast: Shiva Rajkumar, Ganavi Laxman, Aditi Sagar
overview: A former gangster forced into hiding returns to violence after his daughter becomes the target of dangerous political and criminal forces manipulating the justice system for personal power. As revenge and survival push him toward brutal confrontation, hidden truths about corruption and betrayal slowly emerge. The film combines action, emotional fatherhood, crime drama, and political conflict while exploring justice, sacrifice, and vengeance.

title: Dhruva
cast: Ram Charan, Arvind Swamy, Rakul Preet Singh
overview: An intelligent police officer obsessed with eliminating organized crime becomes determined to expose a brilliant scientist secretly controlling a massive network of illegal medical operations and corruption. As psychological battles and strategic manipulation intensify between the two highly intelligent opponents, the investigation evolves into a dangerous game of control and survival. The film combines spy thriller elements, emotional action, investigative drama, and psychological conflict while exploring obsession, morality, and justice.

title: Lakshya
cast: Hrithik Roshan, Preity Zinta, Amitabh Bachchan
overview: An aimless and immature young man drifting through life without direction unexpectedly joins the Indian Army but initially struggles with discipline, responsibility, and self-belief. After personal humiliation and emotional realization, he returns determined to transform himself into a capable officer during the Kargil War. The film combines military realism, emotional growth, war drama, and personal transformation while exploring maturity, patriotism, ambition, and identity.

title: Chak De! India
cast: Shah Rukh Khan, Vidya Malvade, Sagarika Ghatge
overview: A disgraced former hockey player accused of betraying the nation receives an opportunity to coach the struggling Indian women’s hockey team composed of players from conflicting regional and cultural backgrounds. Through intense training, emotional conflict, and collective determination, the team gradually overcomes prejudice and internal division. The film combines sports drama, patriotism, emotional motivation, and social commentary while exploring teamwork, redemption, sexism, and national identity.

title: Guru
cast: Abhishek Bachchan, Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, Mithun Chakraborty
overview: A determined young villager with extraordinary business ambition rises from humble beginnings to become one of India’s most influential industrialists through risk-taking, intelligence, and relentless drive. As his empire expands, he faces accusations of corruption, political conflict, media scrutiny, and moral compromise that challenge both his success and relationships. The film combines business drama, emotional storytelling, and political commentary while exploring ambition, capitalism, ethics, and power.

title: Yuva
cast: Ajay Devgn, Abhishek Bachchan, Vivek Oberoi
overview: The lives of three young men from completely different social backgrounds unexpectedly intersect after a violent political incident on a Kolkata bridge changes their futures forever. As one becomes inspired toward activism and another descends into crime and political manipulation, their personal choices reshape their understanding of power, responsibility, and identity. The film combines political realism, emotional conflict, urban drama, and nonlinear storytelling while exploring youth, corruption, and social change.

title: Special 26
cast: Akshay Kumar, Anupam Kher, Manoj Bajpayee
overview: A group of intelligent con artists posing as government officials conduct elaborate fake raids targeting corrupt businessmen and politicians while staying one step ahead of law enforcement agencies. As an experienced police officer begins closing in on the mysterious operation, the criminals carefully manipulate public trust, greed, and bureaucracy to survive. The film combines suspense, heist strategy, crime thriller storytelling, and dark humor while exploring corruption, deception, and intelligence.

title: Kshanam
cast: Adivi Sesh, Adah Sharma, Anasuya Bharadwaj
overview: A man living in the United States returns to India after his former lover desperately asks for help finding her missing daughter, but nobody around her believes the child ever existed. As he investigates the increasingly disturbing mystery, hidden secrets, emotional trauma, and dangerous conspiracies slowly emerge. The film combines emotional suspense, investigative thriller elements, and psychological tension while exploring memory, obsession, and truth.

title: Animal
cast: Ranbir Kapoor, Rashmika Mandanna, Anil Kapoor, Bobby Deol
overview: A wealthy industrialist survives an assassination attempt, triggering a violent chain of events that deeply affects his emotionally unstable son. Obsessed with earning his father’s love and approval, the son descends into brutality, revenge, and psychological conflict while confronting rival criminal forces and family betrayal. The film explores toxic masculinity, emotional neglect, obsession, loyalty, and violence through intense action, dysfunctional relationships, and morally complex characters.

title: 12th Fail
cast: Vikrant Massey, Medha Shankar
overview: Inspired by real events, the film follows a young man from a poor rural background who struggles against poverty, corruption, and repeated academic failure while dreaming of becoming an IPS officer. Despite social humiliation and personal setbacks, he continues preparing for one of India’s toughest examinations with determination and honesty. The story explores resilience, education, ambition, friendship, and self-belief while presenting the emotional realities of competitive exam culture.

title: Salaar: Part 1 – Ceasefire
cast: Prabhas, Prithviraj Sukumaran, Shruti Haasan
overview: A mysterious and feared warrior living in isolation is pulled back into a violent world when his childhood friend, the heir to a dangerous kingdom, faces betrayal and political conspiracies. As rival factions battle for power, buried histories and brutal loyalties resurface, leading to explosive confrontations. The film explores friendship, power struggles, revenge, and survival within a dark world shaped by violence and ambition.

title: Jigarthanda DoubleX
cast: Raghava Lawrence, S. J. Suryah
overview: A struggling filmmaker is forced to collaborate with a feared gangster while secretly planning to assassinate him under political pressure. As cinema, violence, tribal oppression, and power intersect, the relationship between the two men evolves in unexpected ways. The film combines satire, emotional conflict, filmmaking culture, and social commentary while exploring exploitation, identity, and resistance.

title: Kalki 2898 AD
cast: Prabhas, Deepika Padukone, Amitabh Bachchan, Kamal Haasan
overview: Set in a dystopian future where humanity struggles under authoritarian control and environmental collapse, the story follows a bounty hunter whose mission becomes connected to an ancient prophecy tied to the birth of a child destined to change the world. Blending futuristic technology with mythology from the Mahabharata, the film explores destiny, faith, survival, greed, and rebellion across a visually grand science-fiction universe.

title: Brahmāstra: Part One – Shiva
cast: Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt, Amitabh Bachchan
overview: A young man discovers that he possesses mysterious powers connected to an ancient group protecting powerful astras capable of immense destruction. As dark forces attempt to obtain the legendary Brahmāstra, he embarks on a journey filled with love, destiny, supernatural battles, and hidden truths about his past. The film combines mythology, fantasy adventure, romance, and large-scale visual spectacle.

title: Mimi
cast: Kriti Sanon, Pankaj Tripathi
overview: An aspiring dancer hoping for a better future agrees to become a surrogate mother for a foreign couple in exchange for money that could transform her life. However, unexpected circumstances leave her emotionally and socially vulnerable, forcing her to navigate motherhood, judgment, and personal sacrifice. The film blends humor and emotion while exploring family, dignity, responsibility, and societal perceptions surrounding surrogacy.

title: Shershaah
cast: Sidharth Malhotra, Kiara Advani
overview: Based on the life of Captain Vikram Batra, the film follows a fearless Indian Army officer whose courage and leadership become legendary during the Kargil War. Alongside his military journey, the story portrays his personal relationships, patriotism, and emotional sacrifices. The film combines battlefield intensity with emotional storytelling while exploring bravery, duty, love, and national pride.

title: The Kashmir Files
cast: Mithun Chakraborty, Anupam Kher, Darshan Kumar
overview: A young student begins uncovering painful truths about the exodus of Kashmiri Pandits after listening to survivors recount traumatic memories of violence, displacement, and loss. As hidden histories emerge, he struggles to understand identity, politics, and generational trauma. The film presents emotional narratives centered around suffering, survival, memory, and historical conflict.

title: Article 15
cast: Ayushmann Khurrana, Isha Talwar
overview: A young IPS officer posted in rural India investigates the disappearance of teenage girls and gradually uncovers horrifying realities shaped by caste discrimination and systemic injustice. As he confronts corruption, social inequality, and political pressure, he begins questioning his own privilege and understanding of society. The film explores caste oppression, morality, power, and constitutional values through intense investigative drama.

title: Chhichhore
cast: Sushant Singh Rajput, Shraddha Kapoor
overview: A group of former college friends reunite after years when one of their children suffers emotional trauma linked to academic pressure and fear of failure. Through memories of their youthful hostel days filled with friendship, rivalry, humor, and mistakes, they try to teach him that failure is not the end of life. The film explores mental health, friendship, parenting, competition, and self-worth.

title: Bheeshma Parvam
cast: Mammootty, Soubin Shahir, Shine Tom Chacko
overview: The aging patriarch of a powerful crime family tries to maintain peace within his empire as jealousy, betrayal, and ambition begin tearing the family apart from within. As rivalries intensify, emotional wounds and power struggles trigger violent consequences. Inspired by themes from the Mahabharata, the film combines gangster drama, family politics, revenge, and emotional conflict.

title: Luca
cast: Tovino Thomas, Ahaana Krishna
overview: A police officer investigating the mysterious death of a young woman becomes deeply connected to the life of a troubled artist whose emotionally intense relationship with her hides painful secrets. As the investigation unfolds through fragmented memories and artistic symbolism, themes of love, trauma, loneliness, and mental health emerge. The film blends romance, suspense, and psychological drama.

title: Ayyappanum Koshiyum
cast: Prithviraj Sukumaran, Biju Menon
overview: An ego clash between a short-tempered ex-havildar and an honest but rigid police officer spirals into an intense conflict that affects their families, reputations, and entire community. What begins as a personal insult slowly turns into a battle of pride, masculinity, and social influence. The film explores authority, honor, anger, and human ego through emotionally grounded confrontations and powerful performances.

title: Dasara
cast: Nani, Keerthy Suresh, Dheekshith Shetty
overview: Set in a coal-mining village plagued by alcoholism, violence, and political manipulation, the story follows a carefree young man whose life changes after betrayal and tragedy strike his closest relationships. As grief and anger consume him, he begins confronting the oppressive systems controlling the village. The film explores friendship, revenge, class struggle, corruption, and emotional transformation through raw rural storytelling.

title: Sapta Sagaradaache Ello – Side A
cast: Rakshit Shetty, Rukmini Vasanth
overview: A gentle and selfless young man deeply in love with his partner sacrifices his future after becoming trapped in circumstances involving crime and imprisonment. While separated by fate and difficult realities, the couple struggles to hold onto hope and emotional connection. The film explores love, sacrifice, longing, emotional pain, and destiny through poetic storytelling and deeply human performances.

title: Laapataa Ladies
cast: Nitanshi Goel, Pratibha Ranta, Sparsh Shrivastava
overview: In rural India during the early 2000s, two newly married brides accidentally get exchanged during a train journey due to traditional veils hiding their identities. As confusion spreads across villages and families, the women slowly discover their own individuality, confidence, and desires beyond societal expectations. The film uses humor and emotional realism to explore patriarchy, identity, marriage, and freedom.

title: Mad
cast: Narne Nithiin, Sangeeth Shobhan, Ram Nithin
overview: Three carefree college friends create endless chaos inside their engineering campus through pranks, romance, misunderstandings, and reckless adventures. Beneath the humor and youthful energy, the story captures friendship, insecurity, growing up, heartbreak, and the unpredictability of student life. The film blends campus comedy with emotional moments and nostalgic coming-of-age storytelling.

title: Hridayam
cast: Pranav Mohanlal, Kalyani Priyadarshan, Darshana Rajendran
overview: The film follows the emotional journey of a young engineering student as he experiences friendship, heartbreak, love, failure, parenthood, and personal growth over several years of his life. Through changing relationships and life choices, he gradually matures from an irresponsible youth into someone capable of understanding responsibility and emotional connection. The story explores nostalgia, romance, adulthood, and self-discovery with warmth and emotional realism.

title: Aadujeevitham
cast: Prithviraj Sukumaran, Amala Paul, Jimmy Jean-Louis
overview: Based on the acclaimed novel inspired by true events, the film follows a young Malayali migrant worker who travels to the Middle East hoping to build a better future for his family. Instead, he becomes trapped in brutal slavery under an abusive goat herder in a remote desert. Isolated from civilization and stripped of freedom, he struggles to survive extreme loneliness, physical suffering, and emotional despair while desperately searching for hope and escape. The film explores survival, human endurance, migration, exploitation, and the unbreakable desire for freedom through emotionally intense storytelling and harsh realism.

title: Hanuman
cast: Teja Sajja, Amritha Aiyer, Varalaxmi Sarathkumar
overview: In a small village touched by mythology and faith, a young man unexpectedly gains divine powers connected to Lord Hanuman after discovering a mysterious gem. As greed-driven enemies seek the source of his abilities, he must learn responsibility, courage, and sacrifice to protect his people. Combining superhero storytelling with Indian mythology, the film explores devotion, morality, power, and heroism through fantasy adventure, emotional conflict, and large-scale action.

title: Lucky Bhaskar
cast: Dulquer Salmaan, Meenakshi Chaudhary
overview: Set against the backdrop of banking scams and financial manipulation, the story follows an ordinary middle-class man whose life changes after becoming involved in a risky world driven by money, ambition, and deception. As his choices begin affecting his family, morality, and identity, he struggles to survive mounting pressure and dangerous consequences. The film explores greed, social status, financial desperation, and ethical conflict through suspenseful storytelling and emotional drama.

title: Amaran
cast: Sivakarthikeyan, Sai Pallavi
overview: Inspired by the life of a courageous Indian Army officer, the film portrays the emotional and physical challenges faced during dangerous military operations in conflict zones. Alongside intense combat and national duty, the story also explores the officer’s personal relationships, sacrifices, and emotional struggles as a husband and soldier. The film blends patriotism, action, and emotional realism while examining bravery, love, loss, and commitment to the nation.

title: Munjya
cast: Abhay Verma, Sharvari Wagh, Mona Singh
overview: A young man visiting his ancestral village becomes entangled with a mischievous supernatural entity connected to a forgotten family curse and local folklore. As bizarre and comedic paranormal events begin affecting his relationships and personal life, hidden truths about the spirit’s past slowly emerge. The film combines horror, comedy, mythology, and emotional storytelling while exploring fear, loneliness, family secrets, and cultural beliefs.

title: Maidaan
cast: Ajay Devgn, Priyamani, Gajraj Rao
overview: Based on real events, the film follows visionary football coach Syed Abdul Rahim, who transformed the Indian national football team during a difficult period in the country’s sporting history. Battling political interference, financial struggles, and lack of support, he dedicates himself to building a disciplined and competitive team capable of achieving international success. The film explores leadership, patriotism, sacrifice, teamwork, and perseverance through inspiring sports drama.

title: Jailer
cast: Rajinikanth, Vinayakan, Tamannaah Bhatia
overview: A retired jailer living a peaceful family life is forced back into violence after his son, an honest police officer, becomes connected to a dangerous criminal network. As hidden truths and betrayals surface, the quiet old man reveals a ruthless side capable of terrifying enemies. The film blends stylish action, dark humor, emotional family drama, and revenge while exploring justice, aging, grief, and vengeance.

title: Viduthalai Part 1
cast: Soori, Vijay Sethupathi, Bhavani Sre
overview: A newly recruited police constable posted in a remote forest region becomes trapped between state violence and an armed revolutionary movement fighting for oppressed tribal communities. As brutal operations and moral dilemmas intensify, he begins questioning the system he serves and the true meaning of justice. The film explores power, oppression, ideology, violence, and human morality through realistic storytelling and emotionally heavy conflict.

title: Captain Miller
cast: Dhanush, Priyanka Arul Mohan, Shiva Rajkumar
overview: Set during the pre-independence era, the story follows a man who transforms from a reluctant individual into a feared rebel fighting against colonial oppression and social exploitation. As violence, political resistance, and personal trauma shape his journey, he becomes a symbol of rebellion for oppressed communities. The film combines large-scale action, emotional conflict, and historical drama while exploring freedom, identity, revenge, and sacrifice.

title: Thallumaala
cast: Tovino Thomas, Kalyani Priyadarshan
overview: A young man known for constantly getting into fights falls deeply in love, but his reckless behavior repeatedly disrupts his relationships and social life. Told through a highly stylized nonlinear narrative filled with humor, music, and chaotic energy, the film explores youth culture, ego, romance, friendship, and masculinity. The story balances colorful entertainment with emotional moments and kinetic action sequences.

title: Romancham
cast: Soubin Shahir, Arjun Ashokan, Chemban Vinod Jose
overview: A group of unemployed young men sharing a rented house begin experimenting with a Ouija board for fun, only to experience increasingly strange and terrifying supernatural events. As fear, confusion, and paranoia spread among the friends, their relationships and mental stability begin collapsing in humorous yet unsettling ways. Inspired by real incidents, the film blends horror, comedy, friendship, and psychological tension.

title: Uyare
cast: Parvathy Thiruvothu, Asif Ali, Tovino Thomas
overview: An aspiring pilot’s dreams are shattered after surviving a brutal acid attack carried out by a possessive partner. Forced to rebuild her life physically and emotionally, she fights social stigma, trauma, and personal insecurity while refusing to abandon her ambitions. The film explores resilience, dignity, empowerment, emotional healing, and the strength required to reclaim identity after violence.

title: Sir
cast: Dhanush, Samyuktha Menon
overview: A passionate mathematics teacher is sent to a poorly managed college in a rural area where students are treated as business assets instead of learners. Determined to transform the education system from within, he challenges corrupt management practices and inspires students to believe in their own potential. The film explores education inequality, corporate exploitation, social mobility, and the emotional power of teaching.

title: Good Night
cast: Manikandan, Meetha Raghunath
overview: A simple young man with an embarrassing snoring problem struggles to maintain relationships, confidence, and peace in both his family and workplace. What appears to be a small issue slowly begins affecting his emotional life, marriage, and self-esteem in unexpected ways. Through grounded humor and relatable situations, the film explores insecurity, love, acceptance, and ordinary middle-class life with warmth and realism.

title: SSE Side B
cast: Rakshit Shetty, Rukmini Vasanth
overview: After years of emotional pain, imprisonment, and separation from the woman he loves, a gentle young man attempts to rebuild his life while carrying unresolved grief and memories. As both individuals move through different emotional realities, destiny continues testing their bond and personal growth. The film explores heartbreak, sacrifice, healing, and emotional maturity through poetic visuals and deeply introspective storytelling.

title: Thiruchitrambalam
cast: Dhanush, Nithya Menen, Raashii Khanna
overview: A food delivery worker struggling with family tensions, loneliness, and failed relationships slowly finds emotional comfort through his close friendship with a supportive childhood companion. As he navigates heartbreak, reconciliation, and personal healing, he begins understanding the value of simple human connection and family bonds. The film explores friendship, love, grief, emotional maturity, and everyday life with warmth and realism.

cast: Tovino Thomas, Kunchacko Boban, Asif Ali
overview: Based on the devastating Kerala floods of 2018, the film follows multiple ordinary individuals whose lives become connected during one of the worst natural disasters in the region’s history. As rising waters destroy homes and communication systems collapse, people from different backgrounds unite in rescue missions driven by courage and humanity. The film explores survival, compassion, fear, collective responsibility, and resilience during crisis.

title: Malaikottai Vaaliban
cast: Mohanlal, Sonalee Kulkarni
overview: A legendary wandering warrior travels across distant lands participating in intense combat challenges while carrying emotional scars and hidden burdens from his past. As myths, ambition, betrayal, and destiny shape his journey, he encounters rulers, performers, and dangerous adversaries who test both his strength and humanity. The film combines folklore-inspired storytelling, fantasy adventure, action spectacle, and philosophical themes.

title: Chaaver
cast: Kunchacko Boban, Antony Varghese
overview: A politically connected group transporting an injured man across a tense landscape slowly becomes trapped in paranoia, suspicion, and violent conflict. As secrets surrounding political loyalty and hidden crimes begin surfacing, relationships within the group start collapsing under fear and mistrust. The film explores political violence, guilt, survival, and manipulation through raw tension and emotionally charged storytelling.

title: Virupaksha
cast: Sai Dharam Tej, Samyuktha Menon
overview: A young man visiting a remote village becomes entangled in terrifying supernatural events linked to black magic, mysterious deaths, and hidden rituals. As fear spreads among the villagers, he begins uncovering disturbing truths connected to the village’s past and dangerous occult practices. The film blends mystery investigation, horror atmosphere, folklore, and emotional suspense while exploring superstition, revenge, and fear of the unknown.

title: Eega
cast: Nani, Samantha Ruth Prabhu, Sudeep
overview: A cheerful young man in love is brutally murdered by a wealthy and obsessive businessman who desires the same woman. Reborn as a housefly, he becomes determined to take revenge despite his tiny form and limited abilities. Using intelligence, persistence, and emotional connection, the fly slowly terrorizes the powerful killer while protecting the woman he loved. The film combines fantasy, revenge drama, romance, humor, and emotional storytelling while exploring love, obsession, justice, and resilience in a highly imaginative narrative.

title: Pokiri
cast: Mahesh Babu, Ileana D'Cruz, Prakash Raj
overview: A ruthless hitman working in the criminal underworld becomes involved in gang wars, police investigations, and dangerous conspiracies while operating in Hyderabad’s violent crime network. As rival gangs clash and hidden motives slowly emerge, his true identity and mission remain a mystery to everyone around him. The film blends intense action, suspense, romance, and undercover drama while exploring loyalty, deception, corruption, and survival within organized crime.

title: Businessman
cast: Mahesh Babu, Kajal Aggarwal, Prakash Raj
overview: An ambitious young man arrives in Mumbai with a bold dream of building a criminal empire powerful enough to control the city itself. Using intelligence, manipulation, and fearless violence, he quickly rises through the underworld while challenging politicians, police officers, and rival gangs. As his influence grows, he becomes trapped between ambition and morality. The film explores power, corruption, greed, political influence, and survival in a ruthless urban environment.

title: Athadu
cast: Mahesh Babu, Trisha Krishnan, Sonu Sood
overview: A professional assassin becomes the prime suspect in a political murder and escapes into a remote village by assuming the identity of another man. While hiding among a warm and loving family that believes he is their long-lost relative, he slowly experiences emotional connection and belonging for the first time in his life. The film combines action, family drama, suspense, and emotional storytelling while exploring loneliness, identity, redemption, and human relationships.

title: Oopiri
cast: Nagarjuna Akkineni, Karthi, Tamannaah Bhatia
overview: A carefree ex-convict becomes the caretaker of a wealthy businessman who is completely paralyzed after a tragic accident. Despite their contrasting personalities and lifestyles, the two men gradually develop a life-changing friendship that transforms their understanding of happiness, relationships, and purpose. Through humor, emotional conflict, and personal growth, the film explores disability, companionship, forgiveness, and the meaning of living fully despite limitations.

title: Jathi Ratnalu
cast: Naveen Polishetty, Rahul Ramakrishna, Priyadarshi
overview: Three naive and carefree friends from a small town travel to Hyderabad dreaming of success and excitement, only to become accidentally involved in a high-profile crime case. Their foolish decisions, misunderstandings, and chaotic attempts to escape trouble create a chain of hilarious situations involving politicians, police officers, and criminals. The film blends absurd comedy, friendship, satire, and emotional simplicity while capturing youthful innocence and urban chaos.

title: Agent Sai Srinivasa Athreya
cast: Naveen Polishetty, Shruti Sharma
overview: A small-time detective running an almost unnoticed agency in Nellore dreams of solving major cases and becoming famous. His life changes after a suspicious unidentified dead body case pulls him into a dangerous conspiracy connected to crime, corruption, and hidden networks. As the investigation grows darker, he must rely on intelligence, humor, and determination to uncover the truth. The film blends mystery, suspense, dark humor, and investigative drama.

title: Mathu Vadalara
cast: Sri Simha Koduri, Satya, Naresh Agastya
overview: A frustrated delivery boy struggling with low income and monotonous work becomes trapped in a bizarre murder situation after making a risky decision driven by desperation. As confusion, panic, and criminal conspiracies spiral out of control, he finds himself navigating increasingly absurd and dangerous events. The film combines dark comedy, suspense, satire, and crime thriller elements while exploring greed, survival, and middle-class frustration.

title: Brochevarevarura
cast: Sree Vishnu, Nivetha Thomas, Rahul Ramakrishna
overview: Three mischievous students known for constantly failing exams become involved in a kidnapping plot after a series of misunderstandings and impulsive decisions. As criminals, family pressure, hidden secrets, and emotional conflicts collide, the story unfolds through humor, suspense, and unexpected twists. The film explores youth, friendship, mistakes, ambition, and societal expectations through an entertaining nonlinear narrative.

title: C/o Kancharapalem
cast: Subba Rao, Radha Bessy, Mohan Bhagat
overview: Set in a small town near Visakhapatnam, the film tells four interconnected love stories involving people from different age groups, religions, and social backgrounds. Through realistic characters and emotionally grounded storytelling, the narrative explores loneliness, social prejudice, innocence, heartbreak, and human connection. The film presents ordinary lives with remarkable authenticity while examining caste, religion, aging, and the universal need for love and acceptance.

title: Majili
cast: Naga Chaitanya, Samantha Ruth Prabhu, Divyansha Kaushik
overview: A failed cricketer struggling with heartbreak, alcoholism, and emotional bitterness enters an arranged marriage with a woman who silently loves him despite his inability to move on from the past. As family responsibilities and emotional realities slowly reshape his life, he begins confronting his pain and rediscovering purpose. The film explores love, regret, healing, sacrifice, and emotional maturity through grounded family drama.

title: Ante Sundaraniki
cast: Nani, Nazriya Nazim, Naresh
overview: A young Hindu man and a Christian woman fall in love but struggle to convince their highly traditional families to accept their relationship. To avoid confrontation and emotional conflict, they create elaborate lies that slowly spiral into chaos involving religion, family expectations, and personal identity. The film blends humor, romance, emotional vulnerability, and social commentary while exploring love, faith, family pressure, and honesty.

title: Sarrainodu
cast: Allu Arjun, Rakul Preet Singh, Aadhi Pinisetty
overview: A fearless former army officer who cannot tolerate injustice becomes involved in a violent conflict against a ruthless politician exploiting innocent villagers for power and money. Using aggression and courage, he fights corruption while protecting those unable to defend themselves. The film combines mass action, emotional family drama, romance, and political conflict while exploring justice, anger, and heroism.

title: Arjun Reddy
cast: Vijay Deverakonda, Shalini Pandey
overview: A brilliant but emotionally volatile medical student falls into self-destructive behavior after being separated from the woman he deeply loves. Consumed by heartbreak, anger, addiction, and loneliness, he struggles to control his personal and professional life while searching for emotional healing. The film explores intense love, toxic behavior, masculinity, emotional pain, and redemption through raw and emotionally charged storytelling.

title: Pelli Choopulu
cast: Vijay Deverakonda, Ritu Varma
overview: A lazy young man lacking direction in life unexpectedly forms a business partnership with an ambitious woman after a traditional arranged marriage meeting goes wrong. As they work together to build a food-truck startup, their relationship gradually evolves through emotional understanding, mutual respect, and personal growth. The film explores ambition, modern relationships, entrepreneurship, and self-discovery through realistic humor and grounded storytelling.

title: Kick
cast: Ravi Teja, Ileana D'Cruz, Shaam
overview: A thrill-seeking man addicted to excitement becomes dissatisfied with ordinary life and constantly changes jobs in search of something that gives him a sense of 'kick.' His reckless behavior affects relationships and society until his unusual actions attract police attention for unexpected reasons. The film blends comedy, romance, action, and emotional transformation while exploring purpose, thrill, individuality, and redemption.

title: Happy Days
cast: Varun Sandesh, Tamannaah Bhatia, Nikhil Siddharth
overview: A group of engineering students from different backgrounds navigate friendship, love, insecurity, academic pressure, and personal growth during their college years. Through joyful memories, heartbreak, misunderstandings, and emotional transitions, they slowly mature while discovering their own identities and dreams. The film captures campus life with realism and nostalgia while exploring youth, ambition, relationships, and emotional change.

title: Bommarillu
cast: Siddharth, Genelia D'Souza, Prakash Raj
overview: A young man raised under the constant control of his overprotective father struggles to make independent choices about his own life and relationships. His world changes after meeting a free-spirited woman whose honesty and individuality force him to confront years of emotional suppression. The film explores family expectations, individuality, emotional dependency, love, and personal freedom through heartfelt storytelling and memorable relationships.

title: Taare Zameen Par
cast: Darsheel Safary, Aamir Khan, Tisca Chopra
overview: An imaginative young boy struggling academically faces constant criticism from parents and teachers who fail to understand his learning difficulties. After being sent to a boarding school, he meets an art teacher who recognizes his dyslexia and helps him rediscover confidence, creativity, and joy in learning. The film explores childhood psychology, education systems, parenting, emotional neglect, and the importance of empathy and understanding.

title: Vedam
cast: Allu Arjun, Manchu Manoj, Anushka Shetty
overview: Multiple individuals from completely different social backgrounds unknowingly move toward a life-changing incident that connects their destinies. A struggling rock musician chasing fame, a poor villager trying to earn money, a prostitute dreaming of dignity, and several others confront harsh realities shaped by poverty, greed, discrimination, and survival. Through emotionally layered storytelling, the film explores humanity, morality, social inequality, sacrifice, and the fragile value of human life.

title: Ala Vaikunthapurramuloo
cast: Allu Arjun, Pooja Hegde, Tabu
overview: A middle-class young man constantly mistreated by his father discovers that he was switched at birth with the son of a wealthy family. As hidden truths about identity and family emerge, he enters the luxurious household connected to his real origins and gradually transforms the lives of those around him. The film blends family emotion, humor, romance, and corporate conflict while exploring belonging, self-worth, relationships, and parental love.

title: Rangasthalam
cast: Ram Charan, Samantha Ruth Prabhu, Aadhi Pinisetty
overview: Set in a rural village during the 1980s, the story follows a partially deaf man whose simple life changes after his educated brother challenges the corrupt village president controlling the entire community through fear and exploitation. As political tension, violence, and emotional conflict escalate, the family becomes trapped in a dangerous battle against oppression. The film explores rural politics, brotherhood, revenge, social injustice, and resistance through emotionally intense storytelling.

title: Dear Comrade
cast: Vijay Deverakonda, Rashmika Mandanna
overview: An impulsive and politically outspoken student leader falls deeply in love with an ambitious state-level cricketer pursuing her dreams despite emotional and professional challenges. As anger issues, career struggles, emotional trauma, and misunderstandings begin affecting their relationship, both individuals are forced to confront their own vulnerabilities and personal growth. The film explores mental health, love, ambition, feminism, emotional healing, and youthful idealism.

title: Khaleja
cast: Mahesh Babu, Anushka Shetty, Prakash Raj
overview: A carefree taxi driver unknowingly becomes connected to a remote village suffering from a deadly mysterious illness caused by corporate exploitation and environmental destruction. Mistaken by villagers as a divine savior destined to protect them, he slowly transforms from a selfish individual into someone willing to fight for innocent lives. The film combines fantasy, social commentary, humor, and action while exploring faith, destiny, greed, and human responsibility.

title: Manam
cast: Akkineni Nageswara Rao, Nagarjuna Akkineni, Naga Chaitanya
overview: A young man haunted by the tragic deaths of his parents unexpectedly encounters people who resemble them in another lifetime, leading him into a mysterious journey connected to reincarnation and destiny. As relationships from the past slowly reconnect across generations, hidden emotional truths and unresolved bonds begin resurfacing. The film explores family, love, fate, rebirth, forgiveness, and emotional continuity through imaginative storytelling and heartfelt emotion.

title: 1: Nenokkadine
cast: Mahesh Babu, Kriti Sanon, Nassar
overview: A famous rock musician suffering from hallucinations and psychological instability becomes obsessed with uncovering the truth behind his parents’ mysterious deaths. As fragmented memories, paranoia, and hidden conspiracies blur the line between reality and imagination, he struggles to trust both himself and the people around him. The film combines psychological suspense, emotional trauma, action, and mystery while exploring memory, revenge, identity, and mental instability.

title: Gamyam
cast: Sharwanand, Allari Naresh, Kamalinee Mukherjee
overview: A wealthy and arrogant city man sets out on a long journey to reconnect with the woman he loves, accompanied by a poor but compassionate thief who gradually changes his understanding of life. As the two travel through rural India and encounter suffering, kindness, and injustice, the journey becomes emotionally transformative for both men. The film explores humanity, class differences, friendship, compassion, and self-discovery through a deeply emotional road narrative.

title: Dookudu
cast: Mahesh Babu, Samantha Ruth Prabhu, Sonu Sood
overview: An energetic undercover police officer takes on a dangerous mission to destroy a powerful criminal network while also trying to protect his recovering father from emotional shock. To maintain peace within the family, he creates elaborate lies and staged situations that lead to humorous chaos and emotional complications. The film combines action, family sentiment, comedy, and crime drama while exploring duty, deception, and emotional responsibility.

title: Temper
cast: N. T. Rama Rao Jr., Kajal Aggarwal, Prakash Raj
overview: A corrupt and arrogant police officer who abuses power for personal gain experiences a moral awakening after witnessing the brutal injustice faced by an innocent young girl. Forced to confront his own actions and cowardice, he begins fighting against a powerful criminal system despite enormous personal risk. The film explores corruption, justice, redemption, courage, and social responsibility through emotionally intense action drama.

title: Sye
cast: Nithiin, Genelia D'Souza, Shashank
overview: Two rival student groups constantly involved in campus fights are forced to unite when a corrupt businessman attempts to seize their college grounds for personal profit. Using rugby as the deciding challenge, the students prepare for a high-stakes match that becomes a battle for identity, pride, and survival. The film combines sports action, youth drama, friendship, and emotional intensity while exploring teamwork, rivalry, and resistance against exploitation.

title: A Wednesday!
cast: Naseeruddin Shah, Anupam Kher, Jimmy Shergill
overview: A retired police commissioner recalls an extraordinary day when an unknown man placed bombs across Mumbai and demanded the release of dangerous terrorists while remaining completely anonymous. As the city falls into panic and authorities race against time, deeper questions emerge about justice, terrorism, and public frustration with the system. The film explores fear, vigilantism, morality, and social anger through tightly written suspense and emotional intensity.

title: Udaan
cast: Rajat Barmecha, Ronit Roy, Ram Kapoor
overview: After being expelled from boarding school, a sensitive teenager is forced to return home and live under the harsh control of his abusive and emotionally distant father. While balancing school, factory work, and his secret dream of becoming a writer, he struggles to find identity, freedom, and emotional support. The film explores toxic parenting, adolescence, creativity, emotional suppression, and the desire for independence.

title: Barfi!
cast: Ranbir Kapoor, Priyanka Chopra, Ileana D'Cruz
overview: A cheerful deaf-mute man forms unique emotional connections with two women while navigating love, disability, misunderstanding, and social expectations. Despite communication barriers and emotional complications, his innocence and optimism deeply affect the lives of those around him. The film combines humor, romance, emotional vulnerability, and visual storytelling while exploring unconditional love, loneliness, acceptance, and human connection.

title: Rockstar
cast: Ranbir Kapoor, Nargis Fakhri, Aditi Rao Hydari
overview: A simple college student dreaming of becoming a legendary musician believes that true artistic greatness can only emerge through emotional pain and heartbreak. After falling deeply in love, he experiences loss, fame, emotional isolation, and inner conflict that gradually transform him into a celebrated yet deeply troubled rockstar. The film explores art, identity, obsession, heartbreak, fame, and emotional suffering through powerful music and intense character drama.

title: Swades
cast: Shah Rukh Khan, Gayatri Joshi, Kishori Ballal
overview: An Indian scientist working at NASA returns to his native village to reconnect with his childhood caretaker but gradually becomes emotionally involved in the struggles faced by rural communities lacking basic resources and opportunities. As he witnesses poverty, caste discrimination, and social stagnation, he begins questioning his own identity and responsibilities toward society. The film explores patriotism, development, education, humanity, and the emotional meaning of home.

title: Mahanati
cast: Keerthy Suresh, Dulquer Salmaan, Samantha Ruth Prabhu
overview: The film chronicles the extraordinary rise and tragic decline of legendary South Indian actress Savitri, whose talent, charisma, and emotional depth made her one of the most celebrated performers in Indian cinema. Through the eyes of journalists investigating her life, the story explores her journey from poverty to stardom, her complex marriage, emotional loneliness, financial downfall, and personal struggles behind fame. The narrative combines nostalgia, cinema history, romance, and emotional tragedy while examining ambition, sacrifice, addiction, and the emotional cost of celebrity life.

title: Jigarthanda
cast: Siddharth, Bobby Simha, Lakshmi Menon
overview: An aspiring filmmaker searching for realistic inspiration decides to secretly study a feared gangster known for unpredictable violence and ruthless behavior. As he becomes deeply entangled in the criminal underworld, fear and manipulation begin shaping his work and personal life. However, unexpected emotional shifts and darkly humorous situations gradually transform both men in surprising ways. The film blends crime, satire, filmmaking culture, violence, and psychological tension while exploring fear, ego, ambition, and artistic obsession.

title: Super Deluxe
cast: Vijay Sethupathi, Fahadh Faasil, Samantha Ruth Prabhu
overview: Several seemingly unrelated lives collide over the course of one chaotic day involving infidelity, crime, sexuality, religion, guilt, and personal identity. A transgender parent reconnects with family, a couple hides a shocking accident, teenage boys pursue forbidden fantasies, and a man questions faith after personal tragedy. Through interconnected narratives filled with absurdity and emotional vulnerability, the film explores morality, hypocrisy, acceptance, fear, and human complexity in unconventional ways.

title: Lucia
cast: Sathish Ninasam, Sruthi Hariharan
overview: A lonely movie theater usher suffering from insomnia begins taking an experimental pill that allows him to experience vivid dreams where he lives an entirely different life as a famous actor. As the boundaries between dream and reality slowly dissolve, he becomes trapped in a psychological maze filled with confusion, fear, and emotional longing.
